product_depth.justification: All 7 subcommands fully implemented with meaningful logic. Dependency resolution, phase validation, atomic writes, priority sorting. Not 5 due to hardcoded max_retry in checkStop().

functionality.justification: Every subcommand executes correctly on valid inputs. All error paths tested: missing flags (exit 1), invalid phase (exit 1), missing .harness/ (exit 2), unknown subcommand (exit 1). Dependency resolution verified with synthetic data in both blocked and unblocked scenarios.

visual_design.justification: Help output clear and well-formatted. Error messages actionable. Evaluator.md edit reads naturally. Not 5 because --help lacks per-subcommand usage examples.

code_quality.justification: Clean ES module conventions, consistent patterns, atomic writes, zero npm deps, clear separation of concerns. Not 5 due to hardcoded max_retry, git add -A, and basic commit message escaping.

contract_checks.evidence: All 7 subcommands run and produce correct non-stub output.
Synthetic features.json test: F-002 blocked when F-001 passes=false; F-002 eligible when F-001 passes=true.
feature-select returns valid JSON with feature_id, title, priority, depends_on, eligible, reason.
Unknown subcommand exits 1 with stderr: 'Error: Unknown subcommand: "nonexistent-command"'.
evaluator.md line 48 contains explicit 'ALL changed files' scope statement; 'Documentation-only changes are not exempt'; only skip is zero files.
--help is well-formatted with aligned descriptions; error messages name the problem and suggest usage.
All imports are node:fs, node:path, node:child_process or local ./lib/ paths. Zero npm dependencies.
state.mjs writeState() uses writeFileSync to .tmp then renameSync. progress.mjs appendProgress() uses same pattern.

non_blocking_issues.description: checkStop() hardcodes maxRetry=3 instead of reading config.json max_retry_on_failure.
autoCommit() uses git add -A which could stage unintended files.
Commit message escaping is basic (only double quotes). Shell-special characters could cause issues.

feature_evidence.reason: evaluator.md contains explicit scope statement at line 48 closing the documentation-only exemption. All 4 feature steps verified.
All 6 files exist. All 7 subcommands implemented and tested. Correct JSON output, exit codes, dependency resolution, atomic writes, zero npm deps. All 6 feature steps verified.

test_results.coverage: All 7 subcommands tested with valid inputs. All error paths tested (missing flags, invalid phase, missing .harness/, unknown command). Dependency resolution tested with synthetic data.

codex_detection.detection_method: enabledPlugins in .claude/settings.json, extraKnownMarketplaces in ~/.claude/settings.json, which codex on PATH
codex_detection.detection_result: Codex detected via all three methods. /codex:adversarial-review skill not found in available skills list. Raw CLI lacks review subcommand.
codex_detection.fallback_reason: Codex skill /codex:adversarial-review not available. Raw codex CLI does not expose an adversarial-review subcommand. Fell back to Claude review.

non_blocking.description: checkStop() hardcodes maxRetry=3 instead of reading config.json.

internal_consistency.justification: All 6 files follow identical conventions: JSDoc module comments, node:* imports, harnessDir() helpers, consistent return shapes, uniform UserError handling.

intentionality.justification: Project-specific flag parsing, harness priority enum, dynamic config.json reading, atomic writes for state safety. Builder report documents choices.

craft.justification: Consistent module structure, clear JSDoc headers, uniform error handling, well-formatted output, clean dispatch pattern.

fitness_for_purpose.justification: Subcommands directly callable from Bash tool. JSON output machine-parseable. Error messages self-contained and actionable.
